title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe

seo.imageAlt: Delicious bowl of authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani with naan bread and rice, garnished with fresh cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, creamy, and authentic taste of homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, a beloved Indian classic.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a classic Indian dish featuring tender chicken pieces simmered in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. This beloved curry is renowned for its mild spices, buttery texture, and a hint of sweetness, making it a favorite across the globe. Perfectly paired with naan or rice, it offers a comforting and luxurious dining experience.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with 1/2 cup plain yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1/2 teaspoon cumin powder, 1/2 teaspoon coriander powder, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and lemon juice. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or for best results, up to 4 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon vegetable oil and 1 tablespoon ghee or butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in batches, making sure not to overcrowd the pan. Cook each batch for 5-7 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is lightly browned and cooked through. Remove the cooked chicken from the pan and set it aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of ghee or butter over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onions and cook until they soften and become translucent, which typically takes about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste, green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Sauté for 1-2 minutes until the spices are fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: Add the crushed tomatoes or tomato puree, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, and the drained soaked cashews to the pan. Pour in 1/2 cup of water. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then cover the pan and let it cook for 10-15 minutes. This allows the flavors to meld beautifully and the tomatoes to cook down, creating a rich base.

instructions.title: Blend and Refine the Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully remove the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and green cardamom pods from the sauce mixture. Transfer the sauce to a blender and blend until it is completely smooth and creamy. For an exceptionally smooth texture, you may strain the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan. Discard any solids left in the sieve.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish the Butter Chicken
instructions.text: Return the pan with the smooth sauce to medium heat. Add the previously cooked chicken pieces, crushed kasoori methi, sugar or honey, and salt to taste. Stir well and simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to fully absorb the rich flavors of the sauce. Just before serving, stir in the heavy cream. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro and serve hot.

tips: For extra tender chicken, marinate it overnight in the refrigerator. The yogurt helps to tenderize the meat beautifully.
Soaking cashews before blending is crucial for achieving a super smooth and creamy sauce texture without any grit.
Always add heavy cream at the very end of cooking and avoid boiling the sauce vigorously afterward to prevent it from splitting.
article:
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani: Your Ultimate Guide to a Beloved Indian Lunch Recipe

There are few dishes that evoke the warmth and comfort of Indian cuisine quite like Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani. This iconic dish, with its creamy, rich tomato gravy and succulent pieces of marinated chicken, has captured hearts and taste buds worldwide. While often enjoyed for dinner, its balanced flavors and satisfying nature make it an absolutely perfect Indian lunch recipe, offering a delightful escape from the mundane. If you’ve ever wanted to recreate this restaurant-quality dish at home, or simply learn more about its magic, you’ve come to the right place. Get ready to dive into the world of Murgh Makhani and discover why it remains a timeless classic.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

At its heart, Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a classic Indian curry originating from Delhi, specifically the Moti Mahal restaurant in the 1950s. It’s a dish celebrated for its harmonious blend of tanginess from tomatoes, a subtle sweetness often from honey or sugar, and the luxurious creaminess derived from butter, cream, and sometimes cashews. The "Murgh" refers to chicken, and "Makhani" translates to "with butter," aptly describing its signature richness.

Unlike some spicier Indian curries, Butter Chicken is known for its milder, more approachable flavor profile, making it a favorite even among those new to Indian food. The chicken, traditionally tandoor-cooked (or grilled/pan-fried at home), is marinated in yogurt and spices, ensuring it remains incredibly tender and flavorful. This tender chicken is then simmered in a velvety sauce made from pureed tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and a delicate balance of aromatic spices like garam masala, fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), and cardamom. The result is a vibrant orange-red curry that is as visually appealing as it is delicious, making it an ideal, hearty Indian lunch option that’s both comforting and satisfying.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani requires a thoughtful selection of ingredients, divided into three main components: the chicken marinade, the rich tomato gravy, and the finishing touches.

For the **Chicken Marinade**, you'll typically need:
*   Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into bite-sized pieces
*   Plain yogurt
*   Ginger-garlic paste
*   Lemon juice
*   Spices: red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color), turmeric, garam masala, salt

For the **Rich Tomato Gravy**, the stars are:
*   Butter (generous amounts!)
*   Onions, finely chopped
*   Ginger-garlic paste
*   Canned crushed tomatoes or fresh ripe tomatoes, pureed
*   Cashews or blanched almonds (for thickness and richness)
*   Spices: bay leaf, green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala, kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves)
*   Sugar or honey (to balance acidity)
*   Water or chicken broth

For the **Finishing Touches**:
*   Heavy cream
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped (for garnish)

Having these ingredients prepped and ready will make the cooking process smooth and enjoyable, leading to an incredibly flavorful Butter Chicken that rivals any restaurant.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani at home might seem daunting, but breaking it down into manageable steps makes it surprisingly achievable.

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients in a bowl. Mix well, cover, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours. The longer the marination, the more flavorful and tender your chicken will be.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat a little oil in a pan or grill pan. Cook the marinated chicken pieces until they are nicely browned and cooked through. Set aside. You can also bake the chicken for a healthier alternative.
3.  **Prepare the Gravy Base:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt a generous amount of butter. Sauté whole spices like bay leaf, cardamom, and cinnamon. Add chopped onions and cook until softened and translucent. Stir in ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
4.  **Add Tomatoes and Cashews:** Pour in the pureed tomatoes (or crushed tomatoes), along with the cashews (or almonds). Add the powdered spices (chili powder, turmeric, coriander, cumin, garam masala, kasoori methi). Cook this mixture, stirring occasionally, until the oil separates from the sides, indicating the spices are well cooked.
5.  **Simmer and Blend:** Add a little water or chicken broth, bring to a simmer, and cook for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Remove the whole spices (bay leaf, cinnamon stick). Carefully transfer the sauce to a blender and blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky sauce, pass it through a fine-mesh sieve.
6.  **Combine and Finish:** Return the smooth sauce to the pot. Add the cooked chicken pieces. Stir in sugar or honey to balance the flavors. Finally, stir in the heavy cream and a knob of butter. Simmer gently for a few minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the sauce. Do not boil vigorously after adding cream.
7.  **Garnish and Serve:** Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and an extra swirl of cream, if desired. Your homemade Butter Chicken is ready!

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that truly authentic, restaurant-quality Butter Chicken is all about paying attention to a few key details.

*   **Quality Ingredients Matter:** Start with good quality chicken, fresh ginger and garlic, and ripe, flavorful tomatoes. Kashmiri chili powder is excellent for color without excessive heat.
*   **Marination Time is Key:** Don't skimp on marinating the chicken. The yogurt helps tenderize the meat, and the spices infuse it with deep flavor, which is crucial for the overall taste of your Butter Chicken. Overnight marination is ideal.
*   **Slow Cook the Gravy Base:** Patience is a virtue when cooking the tomato and spice base. Allow it to simmer until the oil separates, indicating that the spices are well cooked and their raw aroma has disappeared. This step builds depth of flavor.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** For that characteristic velvety texture, blend the sauce thoroughly. For an even finer texture, pass it through a sieve after blending to remove any fibrous bits from the onions or tomatoes.
*   **Kasoori Methi is a Must:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) are non-negotiable for authentic Murgh Makhani. Gently crush them between your palms before adding them to release their aromatic oils. It provides a unique, slightly bitter, and sweet aroma that defines the dish.
*   **Balance the Flavors:** Taste the sauce and adjust the sweetness (sugar/honey) and acidity (lemon juice) as needed. A perfectly balanced Butter Chicken should have a slight tang, a hint of sweetness, and a creamy richness.
*   **Don't Boil After Adding Cream:** Once you've added the heavy cream, only simmer the curry gently. Boiling vigorously can cause the cream to split.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a complete meal in itself, but its rich flavors truly shine when paired with the right accompaniments. For an authentic Indian lunch experience, consider these serving suggestions:

*   **Naan Bread:** This is the quintessential pairing. Soft, fluffy naan (plain, garlic, or butter naan) is perfect for scooping up every last drop of the luscious gravy.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice, with its fragrant, long grains, offers a milder counterpoint to the curry's richness, allowing you to appreciate the intricate flavors of the Butter Chicken.
*   **Jeera Rice:** Rice tempered with cumin seeds (jeera rice) adds another layer of subtle flavor and aroma.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based side dish, such as cucumber raita, provides a refreshing contrast to the richness of the curry and helps cleanse the palate.
*   **Pickle and Papadum:** A side of Indian pickle (achar) or crispy papadums can add extra texture and a spicy, tangy kick to your meal.
*   **Fresh Salad:** A simple green salad with a light vinaigrette can offer freshness and balance to the rich Butter Chicken.

Whether you prefer to dip, scoop, or mix, these accompaniments will elevate your Murgh Makhani experience, making it a truly unforgettable Indian lunch.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is undoubtedly delicious, there are many ways to adapt the recipe to suit different preferences, dietary needs, or what you have on hand.

*   **Vegetarian Butter "Chicken":** Replace the chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or even chickpeas. Marinate the paneer/tofu similarly, or simply pan-fry until golden before adding to the sauce.
*   **Vegan Version:** Use plant-based yogurt for marination, coconut oil or vegan butter, and full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream instead of dairy cream. Ensure all other ingredients are vegan-friendly.
*   **Spice Level Adjustment:** For more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the gravy base. For a milder version, reduce the chili powder.
*   **Nut-Free Option:** If you have nut allergies, you can omit the cashews/almonds and achieve thickness by cooking the tomato base longer or by adding a tablespoon of cornstarch slurry at the end. Alternatively, use a small boiled potato, blended with the sauce, for creaminess.
*   **Different Proteins:** While chicken is traditional, you could experiment with lamb, shrimp, or even firm white fish, adjusting cooking times accordingly.
*   **Tomato Type:** Fresh, ripe Roma tomatoes, pureed, offer a vibrant flavor. Canned crushed tomatoes or diced tomatoes can also be used, especially when fresh tomatoes are not in season.
*   **Sweetness Variation:** Adjust the amount of sugar or honey based on your preference and the acidity of your tomatoes. Some recipes even use a pinch of jaggery for a deeper, more complex sweetness.

These variations allow you to customize your Butter Chicken, ensuring everyone can enjoy this beloved Indian lunch recipe in a way that suits them best.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just a dish; it's a culinary experience that embodies the rich traditions and incredible flavors of Indian cuisine. Its creamy texture, tender chicken, and perfectly balanced sweet and tangy notes make it an unbeatable choice for any meal, especially as a satisfying and comforting Indian lunch. By following these steps and tips, you'll be well on your way to mastering this timeless classic and bringing the magic of Murgh Makhani to your own kitchen. Enjoy the process, savor the aromas, and delight in every spoonful of this truly magnificent dish.
